title: Potentilla callieri
taxonomy: Potentilla callieri is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is native to the Himalayan region and belongs to the genus Potentilla. The classification of Potentilla callieri is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the order Rosales. At the family level, it is a member of the subfamily Rosoideae. This classification indicates the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the plant kingdom.

intro: The Potentilla callieri, also known as Himalayan potentilla or callieri cinquefoil, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections. The bioactive constituents of Potentilla callieri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
